quoteFirst: At nightfall we embarked in our canoes to continue our journey, and, as we advanced very softly and noiselessly, we encountered a war-party of Iroquois, on the 29th of the month,
quoteLast: , at the point of a cape which juts into the lake on the west side.
title: A Book of American Explorers
author: Higginson, Thomas Wentworth, 1823-1911

quoteFirst: "The night that the squadron anchored at the back of Berryhead, Buonaparte sent for me
quoteLast: and said he was informed by Bertrand, that I had received orders to remove him to the Northumberland, and wished to know if that was the case; on being told that it was, he requested I would write a letter to Bertrand, stating I had such orders, that it might not appear that he went of his own accord, but that he had been forced to do so. I told him, I could have no objection, and wrote a letter to that effect (a copy of which is here annexed), which your Lordship afterwards sanctioned, and desired me, if he required it, to give him a copy of the order.
title:
The Surrender of Napoleon
Being the narrative of the surrender of Buonaparte, and of his residence on board H.M.S. Bellerophon, with a detail of the principal events that occurred in that ship between the 24th of May and the 8th of August 1815
author: Maitland, Frederick Lewis, Sir, 1777-1839; Dickson, William Kirk, 1860-1949 [Editor]

quoteLast: the British mail steamer arrived from St. Thomas.
title:
The Cruise of the Alabama and the Sumter
From the Private Journals and Other Papers of Commander R. Semmes, C.S.N., and Other Officers
author: Semmes, Raphael, 1809-1877

quoteFirst: I found Uncle Frank seated in the chimney corner,
title: Narrative of William W. Brown, a Fugitive Slave

quoteFirst: 'I, Louis Le Baille, merchant of the town of Pontscorf, on the river Ellé, in the diocese of Vannes, declare with truth that, returning from a voyage to Scotland the 13th of the month of February, 1534, at
quoteLast: , we were overtaken by such a violent storm that the waves covered the vessel, in which were twenty-six persons, and we went to the bottom.
title: Wanderings by Southern Waters, Eastern Aquitaine
author: Barker, Edward Harrison, 1851-1919

quoteLast: , and lay down fully dressed upon a couch, where I fell asleep.
title:
Memoirs of the Empress Catherine II.
Written by Herself
author: Catherine II, Empress of Russia, 1729-1796; Herzen, Aleksandr, 1812-1870 [Translator]

quoteLast: we left the laager in order to climb the hill at      half-past 2 A.M. Having reached Neutral Hill, we left our horses      there and proceeded on foot. It was very dark, and all was still as      death. We walked forward slowly and spoke only in whispers, and yet      our progress was not so silent but that we feared we should be      heard. In the silence of the night the slightest rustle of tree or      shrub sounded loud in our ears, and the thud of our feet on the      loose stones seemed to me like the tramp of a troop of horses. The      enemy, thought I, would certainly become aware of our approach long
title: In the Shadow of Death
author: Kritzinger, P. H. (Pieter Hendrick), 1870-1930; Mc Donald, R. D. (Roelof Daniel), 1873-1949

quoteLast: on the 8th of May, the day that I left Kidal, that I first saw Halley’s Comet.
title: Through Timbuctu and across the great Sahara
author: Haywood, A. H. W. (Austin Hubert Wightwick), 1879?-1965

quoteLast: Essex and Southampton held a parley from the top of the house with Sir Robert Sidney, and surrendered on promise of a fair trial.
title:
Cassell's History of England, Vol. 2 (of 8)
From the Wars of the Roses to the Great Rebellion
